category.label: CASES
category.description: Famous market events and trades — concepts illustrated through what actually happened. The lessons stick.

entries.title: LTCM 1998 — Genius Without Liquidity
entries.subtitle: Two Nobel laureates, leveraged arbitrage, and the fund that nearly broke the financial system.

entries.title: Michael Burry's CDS Shorts (2005–08)
entries.subtitle: How one fund manager bet against subprime mortgages and made billions when the market caught up.

entries.title: GameStop January 2021 — Short Squeeze + Gamma Squeeze
entries.subtitle: A retail trade pile-on broke the short-interest math and cost hedge funds billions.

external_links.title: Staff Report on Equity and Options Market Structure

entries.title: Silicon Valley Bank — March 2023
entries.subtitle: A 40-year-old bank failed in 36 hours from duration mismatch + a deposit run.

external_links.title: Review of the Federal Reserve's Supervision of SVB

entries.title: Volkswagen Squeeze — October 2008
entries.subtitle: Porsche cornered VW's float and briefly made it the world's largest market-cap company.

external_links.title: How VW Briefly Became the Most Valuable Company

entries.title: Black Monday — October 19, 1987
entries.subtitle: Dow fell 22.6% in one day — portfolio insurance amplified a normal correction into a crash.

external_links.title: Report of the Presidential Task Force on Market Mechanisms (1988)

entries.title: Hunt Brothers Silver Corner — 1979–80
entries.subtitle: Two Texas oil heirs tried to corner the silver market and lost it all when CME changed margin rules.

entries.title: Soros Breaking the Pound — September 1992
entries.subtitle: Quantum Fund made $1B+ shorting GBP when the UK was forced to exit the ERM.

external_links.title: How George Soros Broke the Bank of England

entries.title: Volmageddon — February 5, 2018
entries.subtitle: XIV (inverse VIX ETF) lost 96% in one day, vaporizing $2B and revealing crowded short-vol trades.

entries.title: COVID Crash — March 2020
entries.subtitle: S&P 500 fell 34% in 33 days; Treasury market briefly seized; Fed intervened with everything.

external_links.title: Federal Reserve Actions in Response to COVID-19

entries.title: Yen Carry Trade Unwind (2024)
entries.subtitle: How a BOJ rate surprise triggered the fastest global deleveraging in years.

rules_of_thumb.label: Carry trade risk signal
rules_of_thumb.note: Carry unwinds fastest when volatility spikes suddenly

rules_of_thumb.label: Yen as safe haven
rules_of_thumb.note: Opposite of carry funding direction

pitfalls: Carry trades look like free money until they don't — exit is simultaneous and violent.
Cross-asset contagion is underestimated: yen carry funded global equity positions.

entries.title: UK Gilt Crisis — September 2022
entries.subtitle: Truss's mini-budget triggered an LDI margin spiral that forced the BOE to abandon QT within days.

rules_of_thumb.label: LDI leverage
rules_of_thumb.note: Estimate of UK pension LDI overlay at time of crisis

rules_of_thumb.label: BOE emergency purchase
rules_of_thumb.note: BOE backstop announced Sep 28; only ~£19B ultimately deployed

pitfalls: Leveraged duration matching inside pension funds creates hidden systemic fragility.
Unfunded fiscal expansion in a tightening cycle causes an immediate market verdict, not a gradual one.

entries.title: Archegos Capital — March 2021
entries.subtitle: Bill Hwang's hidden swap positions collapsed, costing prime brokers $10B and erasing $100B in market cap.

rules_of_thumb.label: Leverage ratio (estimated)
rules_of_thumb.note: Total notional ~$100B on ~$10–15B equity

rules_of_thumb.label: First-mover advantage
rules_of_thumb.note: Goldman sold $22B first; Nomura waited and lost $2.9B

pitfalls: Total return swaps hide position concentration from public filings and from other prime brokers.
In a forced unwind, prime brokers defect rationally — the first to sell minimizes losses.

entries.title: Terra/Luna Collapse — May 2022
entries.subtitle: An algorithmic stablecoin's circular backing collapsed under selling pressure, wiping $40B in 72 hours.

rules_of_thumb.label: Anchor yield at peak
rules_of_thumb.note: Artificial demand driver that was the Achilles heel

rules_of_thumb.label: Time to collapse
rules_of_thumb.note: From depeg start to LUNA near-zero

pitfalls: Circular stablecoin backing (UST backed by LUNA backed by UST) is reflexive, not stable.
Unsustainably high yield on a 'stable' asset is a warning sign, not a feature.

entries.title: Enron — Anatomy of an Accounting Fraud (2001)
entries.subtitle: SPV manipulation, mark-to-market abuse, and how the seventh-largest US company went to zero.

rules_of_thumb.label: Red flag #1
rules_of_thumb.note: Hundreds of SPVs with no clear purpose

rules_of_thumb.label: Red flag #2
rules_of_thumb.value: Negative operating cash flow vs. positive reported earnings
rules_of_thumb.note: The most reliable accounting fraud signal

pitfalls: Analyst consensus can be systematically wrong when investment banking relationships create conflicts.
Mark-to-market of illiquid/long-duration assets is opinion, not fact — easily manipulated.

entries.title: Dot-Com Bubble — 1999–2002
entries.subtitle: Nasdaq rose 400% then fell 78% — the canonical case of narrative detachment from valuation.

rules_of_thumb.label: Nasdaq peak to trough
rules_of_thumb.note: 15 years to recover the peak

rules_of_thumb.label: Terminal value share of DCF
rules_of_thumb.note: When terminal value dominates, assumptions dominate

pitfalls: Being right about the industry (internet was transformative) does not protect against buying at 100x sales.
"This time it's different" is maximally wrong at peak bubble conditions.

entries.title: Asian Financial Crisis — 1997
entries.subtitle: Fixed FX pegs, hot money flows, and currency mismatches collapsed regional economies in 18 months.

rules_of_thumb.label: GDP peak-to-trough (Indonesia)
rules_of_thumb.note: Worst among the affected economies

rules_of_thumb.label: Thai baht devaluation
rules_of_thumb.note: The trigger peg that broke first

pitfalls: Borrowing in foreign currency with local-currency revenues is hidden leverage on your own exchange rate.
Hot money inflows finance long-duration investments — when confidence reverses, the outflow is abrupt.

entries.title: Flash Crash — May 6, 2010
entries.subtitle: HFT liquidity withdrawal and an algorithmic sell order sent the Dow down 1,000 points in 36 minutes.

rules_of_thumb.label: Duration of crash
rules_of_thumb.note: Recovery was nearly as fast

rules_of_thumb.label: Waddell & Reed order
rules_of_thumb.note: 75,000 contracts with no price limit triggered the cascade

pitfalls: HFT liquidity disappears simultaneously in stress — the moment you most need it.
Market orders in thin markets fill at stub quotes ($0.01) — use limit orders during high-volatility conditions.

entries.title: LME Nickel Short Squeeze — March 2022
entries.subtitle: Tsingshan's 150,000-ton short exploded to $101K/ton; LME cancelled billions in trades.

rules_of_thumb.label: Price move
rules_of_thumb.note: From ~$29K to $101K per ton

rules_of_thumb.label: Tsingshan short size
rules_of_thumb.note: Multiple weeks of global production

pitfalls: Physical production of a commodity does not allow delivery against financial contracts with different spec — basis risk is real.
Exchange trade cancellation socializes losses from those who profited — exchange credibility is the casualty.

entries.title: Fed Inflation Response — 2022–2023
entries.subtitle: Fastest rate-hiking cycle since the 1980s repriced every asset class and toppled SVB.

rules_of_thumb.label: Total hike size
rules_of_thumb.note: Fastest since Volcker 1980s

rules_of_thumb.label: US Agg Bond Index 2022
rules_of_thumb.note: Worst bond year on record

pitfalls: Zero rates create fragility (SVB duration mismatch) that only becomes visible when rates rise.
Stocks AND bonds can fall simultaneously in supply-driven inflation — 60/40 has regime dependencies.
